description: Trigger and behavior cases for test-data design: persona/baseline catalog, per-layer sources, determinism, parallel isolation, PII-safe synthetic data, cleanup/traceability, evolution policy.

cases.prompt: Design our test data setup — fixtures, factories, and seed users — for unit, integration, and E2E suites.

assertions: Catalogs minimal personas and a read-only baseline world with stable synthetic identifiers (two tenants minimum for A/B compatibility).
Assigns per-layer sources: in-memory builders for unit, real-path factories for integration, API-created per-run fixtures for E2E.
Designs worker-scoped namespacing for parallel isolation and structural cleanup with run/test markers and TTL.
Couples seed evolution to schema migrations with a named owner, and hands code wiring to the engineer skills.

cases.prompt: Our staging tests run against a scrubbed copy of the production database. Replace this with something safe.

assertions: Rejects scrubbed production copies as leaky (free-text fields, JSON blobs) and designs synthetic generation as the default.
Routes any claimed need for production-shaped data through human-approval-boundary with a named anonymization technique.
Provides a migration path from the copy-based setup to seeded synthetic data.

cases.prompt: Decide how our product should scope its tables by tenant and how tenant context flows into queries.

assertions: Product data-layer design is multi-tenant-data-architect; this skill designs the TEST data shaped like that design.

cases.prompt: Just give every suite one shared admin user and one big seed file everyone can edit as needed.

assertions: Rejects shared mutable seed data as the root of parallel collisions and order dependence.
Designs read-only baseline + per-test created mutation targets with worker namespacing instead, explaining the failure class being prevented.
